message:
feat(showcase-html): refactor geometry sync into queued coalescing pipeline

Refactor the WASM showcase HTML's resize handling to use a queued
geometry sync pattern instead of inline resize logic:

- Extract queueGeometrySync(reason) to accumulate pending resize
  reasons (user_zoom, dpr_change, container_resize, etc.) without
  immediately recomputing geometry.
- Extract applyQueuedGeometrySync(trigger) to coalesce all pending
  reasons into a single geometry recomputation per frame tick.
- Track lastGeometrySignature (cols x rows @ dpr : zoom) to detect
  when the visual geometry actually changed vs. no-op resyncs.
- Extract setRunningStatusText() for consistent status bar updates.
- Replace inline geometry logic in applyZoom() with the queue
  pattern, eliminating duplicated fitToContainer + runner.resize
  code paths.

This prevents redundant geometry recomputations when multiple signals
(DPR change + container resize + zoom change) arrive in the same
frame, matching the coalescing approach used in the Rust runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
